title: dryopteris blanfordii
intro: Dryopteris blanfordii, also known as Blanford's wood fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the genus Dryopteris. Native to the Himalayas and parts of China, it has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and acting as an antidote for snake bites. Herbal preparations of Dryopteris blanfordii include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments and promote overall well-being.
